domainMap: Kristen is a strategic-foresight professional. This is the TWICE-WEEKLY WILD scan — its entire purpose is to reach further and stranger than the daily scan, into terrain the daily scan structurally never touches. ===HONEST LIMIT=== this engine reads fixed feeds/APIs, not the open web, so it will still drift toward the legible; fight that by rotating into the least-covered corners of these same sources rather than the obvious ones. ===GO WHERE THE DAILY SCAN DOESN'T=== deliberately hunt in: astrobiology & extremophiles, post-quantum cryptography & codebreaking culture, forensic linguistics & language death, glaciology & deep time framing, mycology & fungal computing, orbital mechanics & space law fringes (debris, salvage, mining claims), insect and microbial farming, gut-microbiome/behavior links, prosthetics & bionics, acoustics/infrasound & olfaction science, art forgery detection & authentication tech, numismatics & alternative currencies, cartographic disputes & border oddities, board-game and tabletop economies as social simulators, competitive-sport rule design as institutional experiment, funerary and reliquary practice outside the West, extreme sport subcultures, seed banks & de-extinction logistics, prison design & carceral technology, dialect and accent shift as migration proxy, artisanal/craft revival economics, occult and esoteric revival movements, maritime law and flag-of-convenience oddities, and anything from a non-English or peripheral-geography source. ===WHAT TO REWARD (weakest first)=== absences (a thing that doesn't yet exist), reframings, second/third-order implications nobody has drawn, cross-domain convergences, single instances, peripheral-geography and non-English coverage, and anything that would sound absurd to a normal professional. Bias horizon to H3 (10-30yr). ===VOLUME & RULE-OUTS=== cast a wide net, return MANY candidates (up to the max), include borderline and even hyped items but LABEL them honestly (classification 'Trend'/'Hype', explain briefly in ai_read). NEVER silently drop something — include and label it. Do NOT assign an impact rating; that is human-held.
gdeltQueries: flag of convenience maritime law dispute
